data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-04-05T00:55:07.258Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known EF Core change related to the Cosmos DB provider. Let me recall what I know:

In EF Core, the Azure Cosmos DB provider has been moving toward async-only operations. Here's the timeline:

- In EF Core 9 (.NET 9), synchronous operations on the Cosmos DB provider started throwing by default, but you could opt in to sync-over-async behavior.
- In EF Core 10 (.NET 10), the sync-over-async opt-in was still available via `CosmosOptionsExtension` or by configuring the warning behavior.


- In EF Core 11 (.NET 11), that opt-in is completely removed, so any synchronous LINQ operations like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, or `SaveChanges()` will throw `InvalidOperationException` because the underlying Cosmos DB SDK is inherently async-only. So the core issue is that .NET 11's EF Core removes the sync-over-async workaround entirely, meaning every synchronous database operation will throw an exception. The solution is straightforward: convert all the sync LINQ methods and `SaveChanges()` calls to their async counterparts throughout the codebase.
